description:
You are an automation specialist who wants each new project to come with its own isolated Python
    environment.  
    1. Create the directory “/home/user/python_automation”.  
    2. Inside that directory, create a **Python virtual environment** named “venv_automation” by using
       “python3 -m venv”.  
    3. After the virtual environment is created, generate a concise log file
       “/home/user/python_automation/venv_setup.log”.  
       • The file must contain exactly **two** non-empty lines (no leading/trailing whitespace):  
         Line 1 → `VENV_CREATED=YES`  
         Line 2 → `VENV_PYTHON=/home/user/python_automation/venv_automation/bin/python`
         
       • Preserve the exact spelling, letter-case, plus the absolute path shown above.  
    4. Nothing else should be written to the log file.
    
    Automated testing will confirm:
      • the existence of “/home/user/python_automation/venv_automation/”  
      • the presence of “bin/python” inside that virtual-env directory  
      • the exact two-line content of “/home/user/python_automation/venv_setup.log” as specified.
truth:
Expected file: /home/user/python_automation/venv_setup.log

    Expected log file contents (no extra spaces or blank lines):
    ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    VENV_CREATED=YES
    VENV_PYTHON=/home/user/python_automation/venv_automation/bin/python
    ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    Directory that must exist: /home/user/python_automation/
    Virtual environment directory that must exist: /home/user/python_automation/venv_automation/
    Interpreter that must exist: /home/user/python_automation/venv_automation/bin/python
